ogTitle: Frogs Hollow vs Frying Pan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frogs Hollow and Frying Pan.
heading: Frogs Hollow vs Frying Pan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frogs Hollow, New South Wales and Frying Pan, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Frogs Hollow (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frogs Hollow or Frying Pan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frogs Hollow has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Frying Pan scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frogs Hollow scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Frying Pan scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frogs Hollow or Frying Pan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frying Pan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
